title: Composed dual-stream attention q20/q24 PWL sequential reciprocal datapath
hypothesis: Direct q20/q24 PWL reciprocal LUTs and compact combinational reciprocal dividers are boundary-infeasible in the current composed datapath. Replacing the reciprocal division with a fixed-latency one-bit-per-cycle sequential divider should reduce the physically critical reciprocal logic while preserving the q20/q24 PWL exponent and bucketed reciprocal arithmetic.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Can q20/q24 PWL softmax candidates become physically measurable when reciprocal normalization is implemented as a sequential divider rather than a case table or combinational divider?
include: generate q8/k8/v8 composed attention RTL for q20 and q24 PWL softmax
preserve bucket8 reciprocal rounding semantics from the PWL quality candidates
compute reciprocal with a one-bit-per-cycle sequential divider
align value streams and done to the sequential softmax valid signal
run the composed datapath guard before OpenROAD
measure Nangate45 area, power, timing, and timing debug paths
exclude: quality re-evaluation, because arithmetic semantics target the same q20/q24 PWL candidates already evaluated

remaining_abstractions: This measures a local composed datapath, not a full Llama7B chip floorplan.
The sequential reciprocal unit is fixed at one quotient bit per cycle; future jobs can sweep multi-bit or shared reciprocal schedules if this point is physically measurable.
RTL/perf equivalence for the sequential reciprocal implementation remains a separate gate if PPA is promising.

required_evaluations.objective: Measure Nangate45 PPA for q20 and q24 PWL sequential reciprocal composed dual-stream attention datapaths after compact combinational reciprocal divider boundary failure.
required_evaluations.acceptance_notes: Accept non-ok metrics rows such as flow_failed as explicit boundary evidence; a no-feasible-points outcome is still useful for the q20/q24 sequential reciprocal frontier.

expected_result.reason: The compact combinational reciprocal divider had no feasible OpenROAD points; a fixed-latency sequential reciprocal divider is the next lower-combinational-complexity datapath.
